140:## Notes for the revival

1	# GYC Canada — Context
     2	
     3	GYC Canada (Generation of Youth for Christ Canada) is a Canadian Seventh-day Adventist
     4	youth ministry. This repository is its public-facing **conference website** — bilingual
     5	(English / French), one site covering each year's annual conference plus evergreen pages
     6	(about, team, FAQ, contact, give, volunteer, archive).
     7	
     8	## Glossary
     9	
    10	### Page
    11	
    12	An **evergreen**, individually-schema'd unit of editable site content — about, FAQ,
    13	give, contact, volunteer, archive, and the home page's non-conference sections. Each
    14	Page has its own typed CMS schema (a FAQ Page is a list of Q&A items; a Give Page is a
    15	directions list plus a donate URL), authored through a dedicated `/admin` section. A Page
    16	is **not** a Conference: a Conference is an entity *rendered into* the `/YYYY` and home
    17	routes, whereas a Page owns its own content. _Avoid_: "route" (a route may render a Page,
    18	a Conference, or both), "screen".
    19	
    20	### Section skip
    21	
    22	A Page or Conference view renders a **section** (Speakers, FAQ, Registration, …) only
    23	when that section *has data*: its list is non-empty, or its optional block is present
    24	(`Option.some`). An empty list or an absent optional block is **skipped silently**. Skip
    25	is "nothing here yet" — it is **not** a tolerance for half-filled content: a *present*
    26	item with a blank required bilingual field is still a hard validation error (the `Text`
    27	"both locales non-empty" invariant is never relaxed). _Avoid_: "hide" (that implies a
    28	visibility toggle on present data; skip is purely about absence).
    29	
    30	### Form definition
    31	
    32	A **data** description of one of the site's forms (contact, volunteer, registration) —
    33	its fields, their kinds (drawn from a closed set: required/optional text, email, URL,
    34	literal/radio, checkbox-boolean, array-of-literal, nested group), their bilingual
    35	labels/placeholders, and their validation rules including discriminated-union variants
    36	and cross-field requirements. A generic renderer turns a Form definition into the rendered
    37	form; a generic decoder reconstructs server-side validation from it. The field *kinds* are
    38	a fixed, specified set — a Form definition cannot invent an arbitrary field type. _Avoid_:
    39	"form schema" when you mean the hand-tuned Effect Schema code (that is being replaced by
    40	the Form definition + generic decoder).
    41	
    42	### Submission
    43	
    44	A persisted record of one completed form (a registration, a contact message, a volunteer
    45	signup), stored as its own bucket object (`submissions/<form>/<id>.json`) at submit time,
    46	with an email notification sent *of* the stored record. A Submission is the durable
    47	source of truth — the planned future registrar reads the registration Submission log; the
    48	email is a notification, not the record. _Avoid_: treating a submission as a transient
    49	email payload.
    50	
    51	### Conference
    52	
    53	A single annual gathering, identified by its **year** and addressed by a year **slug**
    54	(`/2024`, `/2025`, `/2026`). A Conference carries a `theme`, a `bible` reference, `dates`,
    55	a `location`, a `tagline`, and arrays of `speakers` and `seminars`. Each Conference exists
    56	in both locales (EN / FR). Conferences are stored as static data; there is no live CMS
    57	backing them (see "CMS" below).
    58	
    59	### Current Conference
    60	
    61	The Conference the site is currently promoting. Selected by date: the first Conference
    62	whose start date is still in the future, falling back to the most recent one. Adding a
    63	new future-dated Conference automatically makes it the Current Conference — there is no
    64	manual "active" flag.
    65	
    66	### Theme
    67	
    68	Two senses, kept distinct:
    69	
    70	- **Theme name** — the conference's title/motto (e.g. "While It Is Day", "A Still Small
    71	  Voice", "Speak"). Stored as `title`.
    72	- **Theme colour** — a single hex accent colour for that year's visual identity, stored
    73	  in the (poorly named) `theme` field. NOTE: this field name is misleading and is a
    74	  candidate for renaming during the revival (see decision log).
    75	
    76	### Locale
    77	
    78	The language/region the site is rendered in: `en` or `fr`. Expressed in the URL via an
    79	optional leading segment (`/about` = English default, `/fr/about` = French). Every piece
    80	of conference and UI content exists in both Locales.
    81	
    82	### Hiatus
    83	
    84	GYC Canada took a **one-year hiatus** — the planned 2025 conference in Montreal was
    85	cancelled and no 2026-prior conference ran. 2026 ("Speak", Calgary) is the **return**
    86	conference. The 2025 entry remains in the data as a cancelled/archived year.
    87	
    88	### CMS
    89	
    90	Conference content is authored directly as **typed TypeScript data** (not through a
    91	content-management UI). A PayloadCMS scaffold once existed (`apps/cms`) but defined no
    92	content collections, was never wired to the site, and has been deleted (see ADR 0001).
    93	"Editing the content" means editing the conference data file and redeploying.
    94	
    95	A future **custom CMS** is planned as a separate project: home-grown, bun-native, and
    96	**database-backed** (live editing without redeploy), in the style of `paulo-suzanne`'s
    97	admin panel. It will not revive Payload/MongoDB. Until it exists, the typed-data model
    98	above is the source of truth.
    99	
   100	### Speaker
   101	
   102	A person presenting at a Conference. Two roles:
   103	
   104	- **Plenary speaker** — gives a main session (morning/evening plenary); listed in
   105	  `speakers` with an `activity` label.
   106	- **Seminar speaker** — leads a breakout seminar; nested inside a `seminars` entry.
   107	
   108	A Conference may have an empty `speakers`/`seminars` list when speakers are **TBD**
   109	(as 2025 was, and as 2026 currently is).
   110	
   111	### Registration channel
   112	
   113	How attendees actually register for a Conference. Two exist: the **external registrar**
   114	(RegFox, reached via the Conference's optional `registrationUrl` Register button) and the
   115	**on-site form** (the form-builder–driven registration form whose Submissions persist to
   116	the bucket + notify, seeding a possible future first-party registrar). For 2026 the live
   117	channel is **RegFox**; the on-site form is built and proven but not load-bearing until/
   118	unless the first-party registrar is finished before signups open. A Conference names its
   119	live channel by whether `registrationUrl` is set. _Avoid_: assuming the on-site form is
   120	the registration channel — it is the registrar's seed, not (yet) the live path.
   121	
   122	### Registration window
   123	
   124	A Conference may define three pricing windows — **early**, **regular**, **late** — each a
   125	`[start, end]` date pair. Windows are optional: a Conference with undecided pricing
   126	(e.g. 2026 at present) simply omits `registration`.
   127	
   128	## Decisions
   129	
   130	Architectural decisions are recorded as ADRs under `docs/adr/`:
   131	
   132	- **0001** — Collapse to a single Bun + React Router v7 app; delete the PayloadCMS scaffold.
   133	- **0002** — Replace React Aria with shadcn-on-base-ui; Tailwind v4 CSS-first; preserve identity.
   134	- **0003** — Effect v4 on Bun (paulo-suzanne pattern); oxlint + tsgo + bun test.
   135	- **0004** — Railway via Bun auto-detect; environment via Effect Config.
   136	- **0005** — Effect everywhere: Effect Schema is the sole validation layer, the Effect
   137	  runtime the sole loader/action path, conform `/future` + StandardSchema for forms, and a
   138	  tagged `Http.*` error taxonomy. Removes `zod` / `@conform-to/zod`.
   139	
   140	## Notes for the revival
   141	
   142	- The misnamed `Conference.theme` field (a hex **accent colour**, not the theme _name_) is a
   143	  rename candidate during the migration — see ADR 0002's per-year theming.
   144	- Public assets are organised as `public/<year>/<locale>/...`. 2026 art lands in
   145	  `public/2026/`. The 2026 hero images supplied are landscape (~1.5:1); a portrait **mobile**
   146	  crop is still owed (desktop uses `hero-title-verse.png`, mobile temporarily reuses it).

1	# Registration form — equivalence spec
     2	
     3	**Status:** authored alongside Branch 6.5 (registration-launch, ADR 0007).
     4	
     5	This is the behavioural spec the structural Form engine's `defaultRegistrationForm`
     6	`FormDefinition` is proven against, transcribed field-for-field from the pre-migration
     7	hand-tuned `registration-schema.ts` (kept in-tree as `registration-schema.oracle.ts`
     8	until 6.6). Every row here is an assertion in `app/lib/forms/equivalence.registration.test.ts`.
     9	
    10	Registration is the **riskiest migration** (ADR 0007, plan §"Riskiest commit"): a 2-way
    11	discriminated union (attendee / exhibitor) with ~10 per-type / cross-field presence
    12	requirements, a `true`/`false`/`on` three-token boolean codec, and an error set where
    13	every failure path must emit a real `TranslationKey` (a wrong/absent key renders blank in
    14	`FieldErrors`). It is also **client-only today** — the registration route action is a
    15	verified no-op (`2026/form/route.tsx:41-43` `yield* Effect.void`); the schema powers ONLY
    16	client-side `RegistrationStandardSchema` validation. So the harness pins it three ways:
    17	
    18	1. **Decoded-output equivalence** over the per-registrant decode (oracle `Registrant` vs
    19	   engine `definitionToSchema(defaultRegistrationForm)`), valid + every invalid variant.
    20	2. **Emitted-`TranslationKey`-set equivalence** (same field paths, same keys) over the
    21	   client collect-all `toStandardSchemaV1` path, plus EN+FR rendered-string parity.
    22	3. **Render-level field-name + default-value parity**: every field name the engine
    23	   definition declares matches a field the registration form renders, and the form's
    24	   `makeDefaultRegistrant()` keys are exactly the definition's field names — the only path
    25	   registration actually exercises in prod (settled #9: RegFox carries the live channel;
    26	   the on-site form is client-validate + render only until the first-party registrar).
    27	
    28	## Scope boundary — what the engine owns vs the route shell
    29	
    30	The registration form is `{ registrants: Registrant[] }` — a **repeating array** of
    31	discriminated-union items, each with conditionally-rendered nested groups (`parent` only
    32	for minors, `volunteer` opt-in) and boolean *radio* groups (`meals`, `firstTimeAttending`
    33	render as yes/no radios, not single checkboxes). A repeating-array-of-variant-items is
    34	**not** in the closed `FieldKind` set, and per the brief's non-goal the kind-set is closed
    35	(not an arbitrary builder). So the boundary is:
    36	
    37	- **The engine owns one `Registrant`'s validation graph** — the discriminator, the
    38	  per-type required fields, the nested groups, the cross-field/boolean codecs. This is the
    39	  risky part the harness pins. `defaultRegistrationForm` is the `FormDefinition` for ONE
    40	  registrant.
    41	- **The registration route owns the multi-registrant shell** — the `registrants` array
    42	  (`getFieldList` / `intent.insert` "Add Registrant"), the per-registrant
    43	  `registrants[n].` field prefix, the minor/parent client gating (the `dayjs` age math),
    44	  the section headings, and the boolean-*radio* rendering of `meals` /
    45	  `firstTimeAttending`. The route's **validation** is derived from the engine definition
    46	  (`Schema.Array(definitionToSchema(def))` wrapped in `{ registrants }`), so editing the
    47	  stored `forms/registration.json` changes what the form accepts with no code change
    48	  (`derive-dont-sync`).
    49	
    50	Render parity is therefore asserted at the **field-name + default-value** level (every
    51	engine field name is a real registrant field; the defaults match), not by routing the
    52	whole bespoke shell through `<FormFields>` (which renders a flat single instance).
    53	
    54	## The registrant field graph
    55	
    56	### Common fields (both variants)
    57	
    58	| name | kind | required key | invalid/format key | notes |
    59	|------|------|--------------|--------------------|-------|
    60	| `type` | variant discriminator (literal `attendee`/`exhibitor`) | `registration.form.type.required` | — | absent/off-list → key at `type` |
    61	| `name` | requiredText | `registration.form.name.required` | (reuses required) | empty/absent/array → required key |
    62	| `email` | email | `registration.form.email.required` | `registration.form.email.error` | `isMinLength(1)` + `/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/` |
    63	| `phone` | requiredText | `registration.form.phone.required` | (reuses required) | |
    64	
    65	### Attendee-only fields (selected when `type === 'attendee'`)
    66	
    67	| name | kind | required key | notes |
    68	|------|------|--------------|-------|
    69	| `gender` | literal `male`/`female` | `registration.form.gender.required` | required-when-attendee |
    70	| `dateOfBirth` | requiredText | `registration.form.date-of-birth.required` | required-when-attendee |
    71	| `parent` | nestedGroup, `optional: true` | — | minors-only; group optional, inner fields required-when-present |
    72	| `parent.name` | requiredText | `registration.form.parent.required` | |
    73	| `parent.email` | requiredText (NOT email-format — the oracle's `Parent.email` is a bare `RequiredString`) | `registration.form.parent-email.required` | |
    74	| `parent.phone` | requiredText | `registration.form.parent-phone.required` | |
    75	| `meals` | checkboxBoolean (rendered as a yes/no *radio* by the route) | `registration.form.meals.required` | required-when-attendee; `true`/`false`/`on` codec |
    76	| `dietaryRestrictions` | optionalText (genuinely-optional; oracle `OptionalString` = `Schema.optional(String)`) | `registration.form.dietary-restrictions.required` | absent key OR explicit `undefined` valid; present-non-string → key |
    77	| `outreach` | arrayOfLiteral (`laws-of-health`/`homeless-carepacks`/`back-to-school`/`not-sure`) | `registration.form.outreach.required` | required-when-attendee |
    78	| `extra` | nestedGroup (always present when attendee) | — | absent → key at `extra.tos` (oracle anchor) |
    79	| `extra.howDidYouHear` | requiredText | `registration.form.how-did-you-hear.required` | |
    80	| `extra.whyAreYouAttending` | requiredText | `registration.form.why-are-you-attending.required` | |
    81	| `extra.whatAreYouExcitedAbout` | requiredText | `registration.form.what-are-you-excited-about.required` | |
    82	| `extra.firstTimeAttending` | checkboxBoolean (rendered as yes/no *radio* by the route) | `registration.form.first-time-attending.required` | `true`/`false`/`on` codec |
    83	| `extra.church` | optionalText (genuinely-optional; oracle `OptionalString`) | `registration.form.church.required` | absent key valid |
    84	| `extra.merch` | arrayOfLiteral (`t-shirt`/`hoodie`/`shirt`/`none`) | `registration.form.merch.required` | |
    85	| `extra.other` | optionalText, `requirePresent: true` (oracle `OptionalText` = `String.annotateKey({ messageMissingKey })`, NOT optional) | `registration.form.other.required` | KEY-MUST-BE-PRESENT, empty string allowed: an ABSENT `other` inside a present `extra` emits the key (the always-rendered `extra` block POSTs an empty `other`); distinct from the genuinely-optional `church`/`dietaryRestrictions`/`instrument` |
    86	| `extra.tos` | checkboxBoolean | `registration.form.tos.required` | single checkbox `value="true"` |
    87	| `volunteer` | nestedGroup, `optional: true` | — | opt-in; inner flags all `optional: true` |
    88	| `volunteer.*` (12 flags) | checkboxBoolean `optional: true` | `registration.form.volunteer.required` | attribute-less checkbox → submits `on` |
    89	| `volunteer.instrument` | optionalText (genuinely-optional; oracle `OptionalString`) | `registration.form.instrument.required` | absent key valid |
    90	
    91	### Exhibitor-only fields (selected when `type === 'exhibitor'`)
    92	
    93	| name | kind | required key | format key |
    94	|------|------|--------------|------------|
    95	| `synopsis` | requiredText | `registration.form.synopsis.required` | — |
    96	| `website` | url | `registration.form.website.required` | `registration.form.website.required` (oracle reuses the same key for empty + unparseable) |
    97	| `company` | requiredText | `registration.form.company.required` | — |
    98	
    99	## Per-type presence requirements (the discriminator filter, oracle `:239-302`)
   100	
   101	When `type === 'attendee'` and absent, each emits its key at its own path:
   102	`dateOfBirth`, `gender`, `meals`, `outreach`, and `extra` (anchored at `extra.tos`). The
   103	groups `parent` / `volunteer` and the optional scalars `dietaryRestrictions` are **not**
   104	required (optional). When `type === 'exhibitor'` and absent: `synopsis`, `website`,
   105	`company`. Attendee-only requirements never leak onto an exhibitor and vice-versa.
   106	
   107	## Three-token boolean codec (`StringToBoolean`, oracle `:50-59`)
   108	
   109	`"true"` / `"on"` → `true`; `"false"` → `false`; any other token (or a duplicate-name
   110	array) → the field's required key. Applies to `meals`, `extra.firstTimeAttending`,
   111	`extra.tos`, and the 12 `volunteer.*` flags (the last `optional: true`).
   112	
   113	## Intentional, pinned deltas (each contained to one field/case)
   114	
   115	1. **`name` / `phone` / nested-text invalid-type key**: the engine's `requiredText` emits
   116	   its single `requiredMessage` for the empty / absent / invalid-type (duplicate-name
   117	   array) cases alike (one required key per text field — the closed-kind-set design,
   118	   `make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`). The oracle's `RequiredString` already does
   119	   the same (its node + key + minLength messages are the SAME key), so for registration
   120	   there is in fact **no** key divergence here — unlike contact's `name` which carried a
   121	   distinct `.error` node key. Pinned as a no-divergence assertion.
   122	2. **Engine field-graph completeness**: the engine adds an `optional: true` flag to
   123	   `nestedGroup` so `parent` / `volunteer` (the conditionally-rendered groups the oracle
   124	   never requires) are present-validate / absent-ok, exactly matching the oracle's
   125	   `Schema.optional(Parent)` / `Schema.optional(Volunteer)`. This is the one engine
   126	   capability the registration harness surfaces (the "engine fixes the harness surfaced"
   127	   pattern of 6.3/6.4); it changes no other form.

item.text: The spec itself claims every row is asserted in the deleted equivalence harness, so I’m going to verify that claim directly in the diff rather than taking it on faith. The important part now is matching final-state tests against what the harness proved before it was removed.

item.text:
**Inspecting changed files**

I’m considering whether I need to read all the changed files in their entirety since there are 24 of them, and some are quite large. The review prompt suggests focusing on the diff, not necessarily the current files. However, my code-review skills indicate I should read every changed file completely. I might need to balance that with the token size limits. I should inspect the sizes of these files to decide how to proceed.

The whole diff has the expected final-state shape: old schema tests and schemas are deleted, new `app/lib/forms/*` modules are added, and contact/volunteer/registration callers are modified. I’m going to read the new form modules and migrated routes in full now; those are the blast-radius center.

1	import { Schema } from 'effect';
     2	
     3	import { Text } from '../content/schema';
     4	import { root } from '../localization/translations';
     5	
     6	/**
     7	 * The structural `FormDefinition` schema (ADR 0007, CONTEXT §Form definition;
     8	 * registration-launch Branch 6). A `FormDefinition` is **data** — the JSON object
     9	 * stored at `forms/<form>.json` — describing one of the site's three forms
    10	 * (contact, volunteer, registration): its fields, their kinds (drawn from a
    11	 * CLOSED set), their bilingual labels/placeholders, the discriminated-union
    12	 * variants some forms branch on, and the cross-field requirement rules that no
    13	 * single-field check can express. A generic renderer (Branch 6.2) turns it into
    14	 * the rendered form; a generic decoder (Branch 6.2) reconstructs server-side
    15	 * Effect Schema validation from it. This module (Branch 6.1) lands ONLY the
    16	 * schema + the closed kind-set + its round-trip / closure tests — the renderer,
    17	 * decoder, and the contact/volunteer/registration field-graphs migrate in later
    18	 * sub-commits (6.2–6.5).
    19	 *
    20	 * It replaces the page-level-copy-only PLACEHOLDER that lived in
    21	 * `content/pages/schema.ts` (Branch 5.1): every consumer (the per-form registry,
    22	 * the bundled form defaults, `Content.getForm`) is migrated to this module and
    23	 * the placeholder is deleted (`migrate-callers-then-delete-legacy-apis`,
    24	 * `subtract-before-you-add`). The `title` / `intro` copy fields are preserved so
    25	 * the placeholder objects still decode and a form keeps a CMS-editable heading.
    26	 *
    27	 * Modelling principles (`~/.brain/principles`):
    28	 *   - `make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`: `FieldKind` is a CLOSED tagged
    29	 *     union over exactly the eight kinds the three forms use — a `FormDefinition`
    30	 *     cannot invent an arbitrary field type (CONTEXT §Form definition). Every
    31	 *     author-facing string is the bilingual `Text` (both locales required,
    32	 *     non-empty). Every validation-failure message is a `MessageKey` — a real
    33	 *     `TranslationKey` validated at the boundary — so a hand-edited definition
    34	 *     can never reference an error key that renders blank (`FieldErrors` renders
    35	 *     each message through `translate()`).
    36	 *   - `boundary-discipline`: a field's submit-name (`FieldName`) is a constrained
    37	 *     identifier, never an arbitrary string, so it is safe to interpolate into a
    38	 *     form-data path; a literal `option` value is likewise a constrained token.
    39	 *   - `derive-dont-sync`: `MessageKey`'s valid set IS the `translations` object,
    40	 *     consulted at decode time — never a re-declared copy of the key list.
    41	 *
    42	 * The encoded form of this schema IS the JSON stored at `forms/<form>.json`, so a
    43	 * definition round-trips losslessly through `encode → JSON → decode` (proven in
    44	 * `definition.test.ts`).
    45	 */
    46	
    47	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48	// Leaf brands — message keys, field names, literal option tokens
    49	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50	
    51	/** The closed set of valid translation keys, consulted at decode time. */
    52	const TRANSLATION_KEYS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(Object.keys(root.en));
    53	
    54	const messageKeyFilter = Schema.makeFilter<string>(
    55	  (key) =>
    56	    TRANSLATION_KEYS.has(key)
    57	      ? undefined
    58	      : `MessageKey must be a known TranslationKey; "${key}" is not in translations`,
    59	  { title: 'MessageKey' },
    60	);
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * A form-validation error message: a real `TranslationKey`, validated at the
    64	 * boundary against the live `translations` object (`derive-dont-sync`). The
    65	 * generic decoder (Branch 6.2) emits these keys verbatim on each failure path, so
    66	 * an off-list key would render blank in `FieldErrors` — it is rejected here
    67	 * instead (`make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`). The brand keeps the
    68	 * guarantee load-bearing past the decoder; the generic decoder (Branch 6.2)
    69	 * hands a decoded `MessageKey` to `translate()` knowing the boundary already
    70	 * proved it is a real `TranslationKey`.
    71	 */
    72	export const MessageKey = Schema.NonEmptyString.check(messageKeyFilter).pipe(
    73	  Schema.brand('MessageKey'),
    74	);
    75	export type MessageKey = typeof MessageKey.Type;
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * A field's submit-name — the key the browser POSTs and the path segment the
    79	 * decoder addresses. Constrained to a JS-identifier-like token (`a-z`, `A-Z`,
    80	 * `0-9`, `_`) so a hand-edited definition cannot smuggle a dotted path, a `[`, or
    81	 * whitespace into a name the decoder interpolates into a form-data path
    82	 * (`boundary-discipline`).
    83	 */
    84	export const FieldName = Schema.NonEmptyString.check(
    85	  Schema.isPattern(/^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$/, { title: 'FieldName' }),
    86	).pipe(Schema.brand('FieldName'));
    87	export type FieldName = typeof FieldName.Type;
    88	
    89	/**
    90	 * A `literal` / `arrayOfLiteral` option value — the token submitted when an
    91	 * option is chosen (e.g. `"attendee"`, `"t-shirt"`, `"male"`). A constrained
    92	 * token (no whitespace, the URL-safe-ish set) so it is safe in a radio `value`
    93	 * and an off-list value is a hard decode error in the generated codec.
    94	 */
    95	export const OptionValue = Schema.NonEmptyString.check(
    96	  Schema.isPattern(/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/, { title: 'OptionValue' }),
    97	).pipe(Schema.brand('OptionValue'));

   125	/**
   126	 * The recursive decoded shape of a `FieldKind` — hand-written so `Schema.suspend`

   128	 * union cannot infer through its own recursion. Mirrors the `FieldKind` schema

   130	 */

   132	  readonly name: Name;
   133	  readonly label: Txt;
   134	  readonly placeholder?: Txt;
   135	};
   136	type FieldKindShape<Name, Txt, Msg, Opt, Bool> =
   137	  | ({
   138	      readonly _tag: 'requiredText';
   139	      readonly optional?: Bool;
   140	      readonly multiline?: Bool;
   141	      readonly requiredMessage: Msg;
   142	    } & FieldChrome<Name, Txt>)
   143	  | ({
   144	      readonly _tag: 'optionalText';
   145	      readonly multiline?: Bool;
   146	      readonly requirePresent?: Bool;
   147	      readonly invalidMessage: Msg;
   148	    } & FieldChrome<Name, Txt>)
   149	  | ({
   150	      readonly _tag: 'email';
   151	      readonly optional?: Bool;
   152	      readonly requiredMessage: Msg;
   153	      readonly invalidMessage: Msg;
   154	    } & FieldChrome<Name, Txt>)
   155	  | ({
   156	      readonly _tag: 'url';
   157	      readonly optional?: Bool;
   158	      readonly requiredMessage: Msg;
   159	      readonly invalidMessage: Msg;
   160	    } & FieldChrome<Name, Txt>)
   161	  | ({
   162	      readonly _tag: 'literal';
   163	      readonly options: ReadonlyArray<{ readonly value: Opt; readonly label: Txt }>;
   164	      readonly requiredMessage: Msg;
   165	    } & FieldChrome<Name, Txt>)
   166	  | ({
   167	      readonly _tag: 'checkboxBoolean';
   168	      readonly optional?: Bool;
   169	      readonly requiredMessage: Msg;
   170	    } & FieldChrome<Name, Txt>)
   171	  | ({
   172	      readonly _tag: 'arrayOfLiteral';
   173	      readonly options: ReadonlyArray<{ readonly value: Opt; readonly label: Txt }>;
   174	      readonly requiredMessage: Msg;
   175	    } & FieldChrome<Name, Txt>)
   176	  | {
   177	      readonly _tag: 'nestedGroup';
   178	      readonly name: Name;
   179	      readonly label: Txt;
   180	      readonly optional?: Bool;
   181	      readonly fields: ReadonlyArray<FieldKindShape<Name, Txt, Msg, Opt, Bool>>;
   182	    };

   184	/** The decoded `FieldKind` (brands load-bearing). */
   185	export type FieldKind = FieldKindShape<
   186	  FieldName,
   187	  Text,
   188	  MessageKey,
   189	  OptionValue,
   190	  boolean
   191	>;
   192	/** The encoded `FieldKind` — the JSON shape (brands erased to plain strings). */
   193	type FieldKindEncoded = FieldKindShape<
   194	  string,
   195	  { readonly en: string; readonly fr: string },
   196	  string,
   197	  string,
   198	  boolean
   199	>;

   201	/**
   202	 * The unique-submit-name invariant shared by every field list: two fields sharing
   203	 * a `name` would collide in the decoded payload and the rendered form, so a
   204	 * duplicate is rejected at the boundary (`make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`).
   205	 */
   206	const uniqueFieldNames = Schema.makeFilter<
   207	  ReadonlyArray<{ readonly name: string }>
   208	>(
   209	  (fields) => {
   210	    const seen = new Set<string>();
   211	    for (const field of fields) {
   212	      if (seen.has(field.name)) {
   213	        return `field names must be unique; duplicate "${field.name}"`;
   214	      }
   215	      seen.add(field.name);
   216	    }
   217	    return undefined;
   218	  },
   219	  { title: 'uniqueFieldNames' },
   220	);
   221	
   222	/**
   223	 * Shared author copy carried by every leaf field: its submit-name, its bilingual
   224	 * `label`, and an optional bilingual `placeholder`. Spread into each kind below.
   225	 */
   226	const fieldChrome = {
   227	  name: FieldName,
   228	  label: Text,
   229	  placeholder: Schema.optionalKey(Text),
   230	} as const;
   231	
   232	/**
   233	 * `FieldKind` — the CLOSED, specified set of field kinds (CONTEXT §Form
   234	 * definition, ADR 0007). A tagged union (`_tag`) over exactly eight leaf /
   235	 * structural kinds; a `FormDefinition` cannot invent a ninth
   236	 * (`make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`). Each carries the `MessageKey`s the
   237	 * decoder emits on that kind's failure paths — every failure has a real
   238	 * translation key by construction.
   239	 *
   240	 *   - `requiredText`    — a non-empty free-text field (the empty, absent, and
   241	 *     non-string cases all emit `requiredMessage`). `optional: true` makes the
   242	 *     field optional-at-key (absence valid, never a missing-key error) while a
   243	 *     PRESENT value is still non-empty (an empty string emits `requiredMessage`):
   244	 *     the cross-field-gated text whose presence a rule governs but which still
   245	 *     rejects a visibly-blank value (contact/volunteer `phone`).
   246	 *     `multiline: true` is a pure PRESENTATION flag (the renderer draws a
   247	 *     `<textarea>` instead of an `<input>`); it does NOT change decode — a
   248	 *     multiline value is the same non-empty string (the contact `message`, the
   249	 *     volunteer free-text fields).
   250	 *   - `optionalText`    — a present-but-empty-allowed free-text field (the old
   251	 *     zod-permissive "other notes"); a present non-string emits `invalidMessage`,
   252	 *     an empty string is always valid. `multiline` renders a `<textarea>`
   253	 *     (presentation only, like `requiredText`). The `requirePresent` flag selects
   254	 *     between the TWO distinct oracle behaviours the kind must reproduce, never
   255	 *     collapse:
   256	 *       - `requirePresent` absent/false → genuinely OPTIONAL-at-key (an absent key
   257	 *         is valid, emits nothing). Mirrors the oracle's `OptionalString`
   258	 *         (`Schema.optional(Schema.String…)`) — registration's `church`,
   259	 *         `instrument`, `dietaryRestrictions`.
   260	 *       - `requirePresent: true` → KEY-MUST-BE-PRESENT, empty-string-allowed: an
   261	 *         absent key emits `invalidMessage`, a present empty string is valid.
   262	 *         Mirrors the oracle's `OptionalText`
   263	 *         (`Schema.String…annotateKey({ messageMissingKey })`, NOT optional) —
   264	 *         registration's `extra.other`, where the always-rendered `extra` block
   265	 *         POSTs an empty `other`, so an ABSENT `other` inside a present `extra` is
   266	 *         an out-of-form payload the oracle (and now the engine) reject.
   267	 *   - `email`           — a required email; emptiness/absence emit
   268	 *     `requiredMessage`, a malformed address emits `invalidMessage`. `optional:
   269	 *     true` makes it optional-at-key but NON-EMPTY-when-present (the empty present
   270	 *     value still emits `requiredMessage`): the `method`-gated contact/volunteer
   271	 *     `email` whose presence a `requiredWhenEquals` rule governs.
   272	 *   - `url`             — a required absolute URL; emptiness/absence emit
   273	 *     `requiredMessage`, an unparseable value emits `invalidMessage`. `optional:
   274	 *     true` makes it optional-at-key but non-empty-when-present, mirroring `email`.
   275	 *   - `literal`         — a single choice from a closed `OptionList` (a radio /
   276	 *     select); an off-list or absent value emits `requiredMessage`.
   277	 *   - `checkboxBoolean` — a `true` / `false` / `on` checkbox-boolean; an off-token
   278	 *     or absent value emits `requiredMessage`. `optional: true` makes an unchecked
   279	 *     (absent) box valid (the volunteer single-checkboxes).
   280	 *   - `arrayOfLiteral`  — a multi-select over a closed `OptionList`; an off-list
   281	 *     element or absent array emits `requiredMessage`.
   282	 *   - `nestedGroup`     — a sub-struct of further `FieldKind`s (the `parent`,
   283	 *     `extra`, and `volunteer` groups registration nests). Recursive via
   284	 *     `Schema.suspend`. `optional: true` makes the WHOLE group present-validate /
   285	 *     absent-ok: a conditionally-rendered group (registration's minors-only
   286	 *     `parent`, the opt-in `volunteer`) the enclosing variant must NOT demand when
   287	 *     absent, but whose inner fields still run their full checks WHEN the group is
   288	 *     present (`Schema.optional(Parent)` / `Schema.optional(Volunteer)` in the
   289	 *     oracle). An absent non-optional group inside a selected variant is an error
   290	 *     (the always-rendered `extra` group); an absent `optional` group is valid.
   291	 *
   292	 * Two ORTHOGONAL optionality axes, never conflated:
   293	 *   - WHICH FIELDS APPEAR for a registrant (an exhibitor-only field on an
   294	 *     attendee) is the enclosing `variant`'s concern, not a per-kind flag.
   295	 *   - PRESENCE-AT-KEY of a field that always appears but whose value may be
   296	 *     absent (the `method`-gated contact/volunteer `email`/`phone`, present only
   297	 *     when the user picks that contact method) is the `optional: true` flag on
   298	 *     `requiredText`/`email`/`url`. Crucially `optional` governs ONLY whether the
   299	 *     key may be ABSENT; a PRESENT value still runs the kind's full non-empty +
   300	 *     format checks (an `optional` email with value `""` still emits its
   301	 *     `requiredMessage`). This is what makes a `requiredWhenEquals` rule's target
   302	 *     model the oracle exactly — the rule re-imposes PRESENCE when triggered, the
   303	 *     field forbids a visibly-blank PRESENT value either way
   304	 *     (`make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`: a blank-but-present required
   305	 *     value is not representable as valid). There is still no "required: false"
   306	 *     knob that would let a present value skip its checks.
   307	 */
   308	export const FieldKind = Schema.TaggedUnion({
   309	  requiredText: {
   310	    ...fieldChrome,
   311	    optional: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   312	    multiline: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   313	    requiredMessage: MessageKey,
   314	  },
   315	  optionalText: {
   316	    ...fieldChrome,
   317	    multiline: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   318	    requirePresent: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   319	    invalidMessage: MessageKey,
   320	  },
   321	  email: {
   322	    ...fieldChrome,
   323	    optional: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   324	    requiredMessage: MessageKey,
   325	    invalidMessage: MessageKey,
   326	  },
   327	  url: {
   328	    ...fieldChrome,
   329	    optional: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   330	    requiredMessage: MessageKey,
   331	    invalidMessage: MessageKey,
   332	  },
   333	  literal: { ...fieldChrome, options: OptionList, requiredMessage: MessageKey },
   334	  checkboxBoolean: {
   335	    ...fieldChrome,
   336	    optional: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   337	    requiredMessage: MessageKey,
   338	  },
   339	  arrayOfLiteral: {
   340	    ...fieldChrome,
   341	    options: OptionList,
   342	    requiredMessage: MessageKey,
   343	  },
   344	  nestedGroup: {
   345	    name: FieldName,
   346	    label: Text,
   347	    optional: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.Boolean),
   348	    fields: Schema.Array(
   349	      Schema.suspend(
   350	        (): Schema.Codec<FieldKind, FieldKindEncoded> => FieldKind,
   351	      ),
   352	    ).check(uniqueFieldNames),
   353	  },
   354	});
   355	
   356	/**
   357	 * A list of fields — the body of a `FormDefinition`, a `nestedGroup`, and a
   358	 * variant — with unique submit-names.
   359	 */
   360	export const FieldList = Schema.Array(FieldKind).check(uniqueFieldNames);

1	import { Result, Schema, SchemaGetter } from 'effect';
     2	import type { Issue } from 'effect/SchemaIssue';

     4	import type {
     5	  CrossFieldRule,
     6	  FieldKind,
     7	  FormDefinition,
     8	  FormVariantSet,
     9	  MessageKey,
    10	} from './definition';
    11	
    12	/**
    13	 * The generic form decoder (ADR 0007, CONTEXT §Form definition; registration-
    14	 * launch Branch 6.2). Reconstructs the server-side Effect Schema validation of a
    15	 * form FROM its `FormDefinition` data — the same validation the three hand-tuned
    16	 * schemas (`contact.tsx`, `volunteer.tsx`, `registration-schema.ts`) express by
    17	 * hand today. Branches 6.3–6.5 migrate those callers onto this decoder behind an
    18	 * equivalence harness; this sub-commit lands the decoder + its own tests.
    19	 *
    20	 * Why a built schema rather than an ad-hoc validator: the existing form pipeline
    21	 * (`parseSchema` / `formatSchemaResult` in `~/lib/effect/form-schema`) already
    22	 * turns an Effect Schema `Issue` tree into conform's `{ formErrors, fieldErrors }`
    23	 * buckets keyed by `formatPath`. By compiling a `FormDefinition` into a real
    24	 * `Schema.Struct`, the decoder reuses that boundary verbatim — the generated
    25	 * codec emits an `Issue` tree exactly like the hand-written schemas do, so the
    26	 * error PATHS and KEYS the harness asserts come from one shared mechanism
    27	 * (`derive-dont-sync`, `subtract-before-you-add`). The decoder does not re-invent
    28	 * path serialization or message bucketing.
    29	 *
    30	 * Behaviour the generated schema reproduces, transcribed from the hand-tuned
    31	 * siblings so the migration is byte-equivalent (the annotation idiom is fixed by
    32	 * `contact.tsx:47-109` / `registration-schema.ts:69-114`):
    33	 *   - a leaf's empty-string / absent / invalid-type cases all emit a real
    34	 *     `MessageKey` (`.check(isMinLength(1, { message }))` for empty, a node-level
    35	 *     `.annotate({ message })` for the invalid type a duplicate field name POSTs
    36	 *     as an array, `.annotateKey({ messageMissingKey })` for the absent key);
    37	 *   - a `literal` / `arrayOfLiteral` decodes against its closed `OptionList`;
    38	 *   - a `checkboxBoolean` is the `true` / `false` / `on` three-token codec
    39	 *     (`registration-schema.ts:50-59`), `optional: true` making an unchecked
    40	 *     (absent) box valid;
    41	 *   - a discriminated `variant` set models its variant-specific fields as
    42	 *     struct-level optionals and requires them — at their own field paths — via a
    43	 *     struct-level filter keyed on the discriminator (the attendee/exhibitor
    44	 *     split of `registration-schema.ts:239-302`);
    45	 *   - a `requiredWhenEquals` cross-field rule requires its `target` (at the
    46	 *     target's path) when its `when` field equals one of `equals` (the
    47	 *     `method`-gated email/phone requirement of `contact.tsx:91-109`).
    48	 *
    49	 * `make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`: the `FormDefinition` boundary already
    50	 * proved every `MessageKey` is a real `TranslationKey` and every `FieldName` is a
    51	 * safe identifier, so this module interpolates names into paths and hands keys to
    52	 * the issue tree with no further guarding (`boundary-discipline` — the validation
    53	 * lives at the schema boundary, not here).
    54	 */
    55	
    56	/** A decoded form payload — a flat/nested record of field names to decoded values. */
    57	export type DecodedForm = Record<string, unknown>;
    58	
    59	/**
    60	 * The string-or-absent value the browser submits for one field, before any
    61	 * boolean/literal decoding. A duplicate field name POSTs an array, which the
    62	 * hand-tuned schemas treat as an invalid type — so the input schema accepts
    63	 * `unknown` and each leaf's node-level message labels the invalid-type path.
    64	 */
    65	type Encoded = Record<string, unknown>;
    66	
    67	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68	// Leaf codecs — one per FieldKind, mirroring the hand-tuned annotation idiom
    69	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70	
    71	/** Required free-text: empty, absent, and non-string all emit `message`. */
    72	const requiredString = (message: MessageKey) =>
    73	  Schema.String.annotate({ message })
    74	    .check(Schema.isMinLength(1, { message }))
    75	    .annotateKey({ messageMissingKey: message });
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * Genuinely-optional free-text: an empty string, an ABSENT key, and an explicit
    79	 * `undefined` are all valid; a present non-string emits `message`. Realizes the
    80	 * former hand-tuned `OptionalString` (`Schema.optional(Schema.String…)`, the
    81	 * registration oracle retired in Branch 6.6) — registration's `church` /
    82	 * `instrument` / `dietaryRestrictions`.
    83	 *
    84	 * `Schema.optional` (not `optionalKey`) so an explicit `undefined` is accepted too,
    85	 * matching that `OptionalString` exactly on the values the form actually submits
    86	 * (absent / valid string). The wrapper is `.annotate({ message })` so a present
    87	 * non-string (a duplicate-name ARRAY — an out-of-form payload) maps to the field's
    88	 * real `MessageKey` instead of `Schema.optional`'s default
    89	 * `"Expected string | undefined, got …"` union-mismatch text (which would render
    90	 * BLANK in `FieldErrors`). The former oracle left this edge un-annotated; relabeling
    91	 * it is strictly safer and was never observed in the equivalence corpus (the form
    92	 * submits absent or a string for a single text field). The same wrapper-annotation
    93	 * idiom as the `optional: true` text/email/url path below. This is the
    94	 * `requirePresent` absent/false branch.
    95	 */
    96	const optionalText = (message: MessageKey) =>
    97	  Schema.optional(Schema.String.annotate({ message })).annotate({ message });
    98	
    99	/**
   100	 * Key-must-be-present, empty-string-allowed free-text: a present non-string emits
   101	 * `message`; an empty string is valid; an ABSENT key emits `message` (via
   102	 * `messageMissingKey`). Realizes the former hand-tuned `OptionalText`
   103	 * (`Schema.String…annotateKey({ messageMissingKey })`, NOT optional; the
   104	 * registration oracle retired in Branch 6.6) — registration's `extra.other`. The
   105	 * two former behaviours must NOT collapse onto one engine kind (the always-rendered
   106	 * `extra` block POSTs an empty `other`, so an absent `other` inside a present
   107	 * `extra` is the out-of-form payload that schema rejects). Selected by the
   108	 * `optionalText` kind's `requirePresent: true` flag.
   109	 */

   111	  Schema.String.annotate({ message }).annotateKey({ messageMissingKey: message });
   112	
   113	/** Required email: empty/absent emit `requiredMessage`, malformed emits `invalidMessage`. */
   114	const email = (requiredMessage: MessageKey, invalidMessage: MessageKey) =>
   115	  Schema.String.annotate({ message: invalidMessage })

   117	      Schema.isMinLength(1, { message: requiredMessage }),
   118	      // The former zod `.email()`: a basic, permissive email shape.
   119	      Schema.isPattern(/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/, { message: invalidMessage }),
   120	    )
   121	    .annotateKey({ messageMissingKey: requiredMessage });
   122	
   123	/** Required absolute URL: empty/absent emit `requiredMessage`, unparseable emits `invalidMessage`. */
   124	const url = (requiredMessage: MessageKey, invalidMessage: MessageKey) =>
   125	  Schema.String.annotate({ message: invalidMessage })
   126	    .check(
   127	      Schema.isMinLength(1, { message: requiredMessage }),
   128	      Schema.makeFilter((value: string) => URL.canParse(value), {
   129	        message: invalidMessage,
   130	      }),
   131	    )
   132	    .annotateKey({ messageMissingKey: requiredMessage });

   134	/** Required single choice from a closed option set: off-list/absent emit `message`. */

   137	  message: MessageKey,
   138	) =>
   139	  Schema.Literals(options.map((option) => option.value))
   140	    .annotate({ message })
   141	    .annotateKey({ messageMissingKey: message });

   143	/**

   153	        decode: SchemaGetter.transform((value) => value !== 'false'),
   154	        encode: SchemaGetter.transform((value) => (value ? 'true' : 'false')),
   155	      }),
   156	    )
   157	    .annotate({ message });

   159	/** Required multi-select over a closed option set: an off-list element emits `message`. */

   161	  options: ReadonlyArray<{ readonly value: string }>,
   162	  message: MessageKey,

   164	  Schema.mutable(

   166	      Schema.Literals(options.map((option) => option.value)).annotate({
   167	        message,
   168	      }),
   169	    ),
   170	  ).annotateKey({ messageMissingKey: message });

   172	/**

   206	/**

   224	    return [field.name, Schema.optionalKey(stringToBoolean(field.requiredMessage))];
   225	  }

   227	  // keeps its full non-empty + format codec for a PRESENT value — the empty
   228	  // present value still emits `requiredMessage`. This is the cross-field-gated
   229	  // `email`/`phone` of contact/volunteer (`Schema.optional(Email)` in the
   230	  // oracle, where `Email` itself enforces `isMinLength(1)`): the rule governs
   231	  // presence, the field forbids a blank present value. `Schema.optional` (not
   232	  // `optionalKey`) so an explicit `undefined` is also accepted, matching the
   233	  // oracle wrapper. The wrapper is annotated with the field's INVALID-TYPE
   234	  // message (`invalidMessage` for email/url, `requiredMessage` for text) so a
   235	  // duplicate-name ARRAY value maps to a real key instead of the `optional`
   236	  // wrapper's default union-mismatch text — exactly the oracle's
   237	  // `Schema.optional(Email).annotate({ message })`
   238	  // (`contact.tsx`'s `email`/`phone` wrappers).
   239	  if (
   240	    (field._tag === 'requiredText' ||
   241	      field._tag === 'email' ||
   242	      field._tag === 'url') &&
   243	    field.optional === true
   244	  ) {
   245	    const invalidTypeMessage =
   246	      field._tag === 'requiredText' ? field.requiredMessage : field.invalidMessage;
   247	    return [
   248	      field.name,
   249	      Schema.optional(
   250	        fieldToRequiredSchema(field) as Schema.Codec<unknown, unknown>,
   251	      ).annotate({ message: invalidTypeMessage }),
   252	    ];
   253	  }
   254	  return [field.name, fieldToRequiredSchema(field)];
   255	};
   256	
   257	/** Build the `Schema.Struct` field map for a list of fields. */
   258	const buildStructFields = (
   259	  fields: ReadonlyArray<FieldKind>,
   260	): Record<string, Schema.Top> => {
   261	  const entries: Record<string, Schema.Top> = {};
   262	  for (const field of fields) {
   263	    const [name, schema] = fieldToStructEntry(field);
   264	    entries[name] = schema;
   265	  }
   266	  return entries;
   267	};
   268	
   269	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   270	// Variants — optional struct fields + a discriminator-keyed presence filter
   271	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   272	
   273	/**
   274	 * A variant's fields are modelled as struct-level optionals so the off-variant
   275	 * never demands them; the presence requirement is re-imposed (at each field's own
   276	 * path) by {@link makeVariantFilter}. A present variant field still runs its own
   277	 * format/literal checks. Mirrors `registration-schema.ts:216-238`.
   278	 */
   279	const buildVariantOptionalFields = (
   280	  variant: FormVariantSet,
   281	): Record<string, Schema.Top> => {
   282	  const entries: Record<string, Schema.Top> = {};
   283	  // The discriminator itself is always required (its absent/off-list value
   284	  // attributes to the discriminator field), like `Registrant.type`.
   285	  entries[variant.discriminator] = Schema.Literals(
   286	    variant.options.map((option) => option.value),
   287	  )
   288	    .annotate({ message: variant.requiredMessage })
   289	    .annotateKey({ messageMissingKey: variant.requiredMessage });
   290	  for (const branch of variant.variants) {
   291	    for (const field of branch.fields) {
   292	      const [name, schema] = fieldToStructEntry(field);
   293	      // Already optional (optionalText / optional checkbox) → keep as-is; an
   294	      // inherently-required leaf is wrapped so the off-variant doesn't demand it.
   295	      entries[name] = Schema.optional(schema as Schema.Codec<unknown, unknown>);
   296	    }
   297	  }
   298	  return entries;
   299	};
   300	
   301	/** One presence issue a struct-level predicate reports, at a field path. */
   302	type PresenceIssue = {
   303	  readonly path: ReadonlyArray<PropertyKey>;
   304	  readonly issue: string;
   305	};
   306	
   307	/**
   308	 * The required `MessageKey` a presence-requirable LEAF emits when absent. A
   309	 * `nestedGroup` (handled by {@link groupPresenceIssue}) and an `optionalText`
   310	 * (never presence-required) are excluded from the input type — they have no
   311	 * single required key to return.
   312	 */
   313	const requiredMessageOf = (
   314	  field: Extract<FieldKind, { requiredMessage: MessageKey }>,
   315	): string => field.requiredMessage;
   316	
   317	/**
   318	 * The presence issue an ABSENT selected-variant `nestedGroup` surfaces: a real
   319	 * key at the group's FIRST presence-requirable inner field path (e.g.
   320	 * `['extra', <first required inner field>]`), mirroring the registration
   321	 * oracle's `['extra', 'tos']` (`registration-schema.ts:274-279`) — a selected
   322	 * attendee branch that omits the whole `extra` group is an error, not a success
   323	 * (`make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`). An empty group (no presence-
   324	 * requirable inner field) cannot anchor a key, so it imposes no group-level
   325	 * presence — its own (absent) inner fields carry no requirement anyway.
   326	 */
   327	const groupPresenceIssue = (
   328	  group: Extract<FieldKind, { _tag: 'nestedGroup' }>,
   329	): PresenceIssue | undefined => {
   330	  for (const inner of group.fields) {
   331	    if (!isPresenceRequirableLeaf(inner)) continue;
   332	    return { path: [group.name, inner.name], issue: requiredMessageOf(inner) };
   333	  }
   334	  return undefined;
   335	};
   336	
   337	/**
   338	 * A leaf whose PRESENCE a variant/group can require when its branch is selected:
   339	 * everything except `optionalText` (empty-allowed), an `optional: true` leaf
   340	 * (checkbox / scalar — legitimately absent), and a `nestedGroup` (its own
   341	 * presence is anchored by {@link groupPresenceIssue}, not a single key). The
   342	 * survivors all carry a `requiredMessage`, so the narrowed type is the input to
   343	 * {@link requiredMessageOf}.
   344	 */
   345	const isPresenceRequirableLeaf = (
   346	  field: FieldKind,
   347	): field is Extract<FieldKind, { requiredMessage: MessageKey }> => {
   348	  if (field._tag === 'optionalText' || field._tag === 'nestedGroup') {
   349	    return false;
   350	  }
   351	  if ('optional' in field && field.optional === true) return false;
   352	  return true;
   353	};
   354	
   355	/**
   356	 * The presence requirements a discriminated `variant` imposes on the SELECTED
   357	 * branch's fields — at their own paths — collected (not aborted) so a payload
   358	 * missing several branch fields surfaces them all at once, exactly as the
   359	 * oracle's single accumulating struct filter does

item.text: A few things already look promising: the decoder compiles to Effect Schema and reuses `formatSchemaResult`, so it appears to avoid a separate error-bucketing system. I’m continuing through the recursive variant/presence code because that’s where subtle “one missing field masks another” bugs would hide.

   363	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   367	/** A non-empty list of variants is meaningless below two members. */
   368	const atLeastTwoVariants = Schema.makeFilter<ReadonlyArray<unknown>>(
   369	  (variants) =>
   370	    variants.length >= 2
   371	      ? undefined
   372	      : 'a variant set must offer at least two variants',
   373	  { title: 'FormVariantSet.variants' },
   374	);
   375	
   376	/**
   377	 * The discriminator's `options` and its `variants` describe ONE closed
   378	 * value-set seen from two sides: each selectable option MUST have exactly one
   379	 * variant branch, and each branch MUST be reachable by exactly one option
   380	 * (`make-impossible-states-unrepresentable`). Without this bijection a
   381	 * definition could decode with a selectable radio that branches to no fields
   382	 * (an option with no variant), an unreachable branch (a variant with no
   383	 * option), or a duplicate value collapsing two entries on either side — all
   384	 * impossible states the renderer/decoder (Branch 6.2) would have to special-case
   385	 * downstream. Asserted as a struct-level filter (it needs both arrays) over the
   386	 * decoded `OptionValue` tokens, mirroring `DateRange`'s ordered-pair filter in
   387	 * `content/schema.ts`.
   388	 */
   389	const variantsMatchOptions = Schema.makeFilter<{
   390	  readonly options: ReadonlyArray<{ readonly value: string }>;
   391	  readonly variants: ReadonlyArray<{ readonly value: string }>;
   392	}>(
   393	  ({ options, variants }) => {
   394	    const optionValues = options.map((option) => option.value);
   395	    const variantValues = variants.map((variant) => variant.value);
   396	
   397	    const optionSet = new Set(optionValues);
   398	    if (optionSet.size !== optionValues.length) {
   399	      return 'discriminator options must not repeat a value';
   400	    }
   401	    const variantSet = new Set(variantValues);
   402	    if (variantSet.size !== variantValues.length) {
   403	      return 'variant branches must not repeat a value';
   404	    }
   405	
   406	    for (const value of optionValues) {
   407	      if (!variantSet.has(value)) {
   408	        return `discriminator option "${value}" has no matching variant branch`;
   409	      }
   410	    }
   411	    for (const value of variantValues) {
   412	      if (!optionSet.has(value)) {
   413	        return `variant branch "${value}" has no matching discriminator option`;
   414	      }
   415	    }
   416	    return undefined;
   417	  },
   418	  { title: 'FormVariantSet.variantsMatchOptions' },
   419	);
   420	
   421	/**
   422	 * A single variant of a discriminated form section: the discriminator `value`
   423	 * that selects it (e.g. `"attendee"`), its bilingual `label` (the radio option),
   424	 * and the `fields` that exist ONLY in that variant. Registration's
   425	 * attendee/exhibitor split is two `FormVariant`s.

   436	 * `discriminator` submit-name, the closed set of `options` it selects between,
   437	 * and the `requiredMessage` for an absent/off-list discriminator value. The
   438	 * common fields of the section live in the enclosing `FormDefinition.fields`; the
   439	 * variant-specific fields live in each `FormVariant`. A form with no

   441	 * cross-field rule over a `literal`, not a structural variant — only registration
   442	 * structurally varies its field set).
   443	 */

   445	  discriminator: FieldName,
   446	  options: OptionList,
   447	  requiredMessage: MessageKey,
   448	  variants: Schema.Array(FormVariant).check(atLeastTwoVariants),
   449	}).check(variantsMatchOptions);
   450	export type FormVariantSet = typeof FormVariantSet.Type;

   462	  { title: 'requiredWhenEquals.equals' },
   463	);
   464	
   465	/**
   466	 * A CLOSED set of cross-field requirement rules — the validity no single-field
   467	 * check can express (CONTEXT §Form definition). One kind today:
   468	 *
   469	 *   - `requiredWhenEquals` — the `target` field is required when the `when` field
   470	 *     equals one of `equals` (e.g. contact's email is required when `method` is
   471	 *     `email` or `both`); the failure emits `message` at the `target` path.
   472	 *

   480	    equals: Schema.Array(OptionValue).check(nonEmptyEquals),
   481	    target: FieldName,

   483	  },

   505	});

    19	 * hand-tuned `registration-schema.oracle.ts`). That harness served its purpose once
    20	 * green and was RETIRED with the oracle in 6.6 (ADR 0007: "the oracle is removed
    21	 * once registration is fully migrated") — the engine's decode behaviour is now
    22	 * covered directly by `decode.test.ts` / `definition.test.ts`.
    23	 *
    24	 * These render-parity tests OUTLIVE the oracle: they assert the LIVE
    25	 * `<RegistrationForm>` (the bespoke `{ registrants: [...] }` shell, NOT the generic
    26	 * `FormFields` renderer that `render.test.tsx` covers) emits submit-`name=`s that
    27	 * match `defaultRegistrationForm`'s field graph exactly, and that the form's REAL
    28	 * `makeDefaultRegistrant` seeds a key for every rendered field name
    29	 * (`derive-dont-sync`). This is the only path registration exercises in prod
    30	 * (settled #9: RegFox is the live channel; the on-site form's client validate +
    31	 * render is what runs), so a control with the wrong `name` or no `name` — the class
    32	 * of bug the pre-existing `cameraOperator`/`photographer` mix-up was — is what these
    33	 * guard. See `docs/forms/registration-spec.md`.
    34	 */
    35	
    36	type GroupishField = {
    37	  readonly _tag: string;
    38	  readonly name: string;
    39	  readonly optional?: boolean;
    40	  readonly fields?: ReadonlyArray<unknown>;
    41	};
    42	
    43	/**
    44	 * Collect every field name a list declares, by group path.
    45	 *
    46	 * `descendOptional` chooses how `optional: true` groups (the minors-only
    47	 * `parent`, the opt-in `volunteer`) are treated:
    48	 *   - `false` (RENDER view): an optional group is NOT descended into — the live
    49	 *     form renders its inner controls only when the group is shown, so at the
    50	 *     top-level render only the group NAME could appear. A non-optional group
    51	 *     (`extra`, always rendered for an attendee) IS descended so each inner
    52	 *     control's submit-name is pinned.
    53	 *   - `true` (DEFAULT-VALUE view): every group is descended, including the optional
    54	 *     ones — the form's `makeDefaultRegistrant` seeds a key for EVERY field name
    55	 *     (including `volunteer.cameraOperator`, `volunteer.photographer`), so the
    56	 *     default object must carry each. This is the `derive-dont-sync` check: the
    57	 *     conform `name={volunteer.cameraOperator.name}` accessors the form renders are
    58	 *     keyed off this default, so a default that omits a name (or a name the default
    59	 *     omits) is the drift this test forbids.
    60	 */
    61	const collectNames = (
    62	  fields: ReadonlyArray<GroupishField>,
    63	  descendOptional: boolean,
    64	  prefix = '',
    65	): string[] =>
    66	  fields.flatMap((field) =>
    67	    field._tag === 'nestedGroup' &&
    68	    field.fields &&
    69	    (descendOptional || field.optional !== true)
    70	      ? collectNames(
    71	          field.fields as ReadonlyArray<GroupishField>,
    72	          descendOptional,
    73	          `${prefix}${field.name}.`,
    74	        )
    75	      : [`${prefix}${field.name}`],
    76	  );
    77	
    78	/**
    79	 * Render the live `<RegistrationForm>` to an HTML string (the real render path —
    80	 * `react-router`'s `Form` + conform `useForm` + the bespoke shell), and return the
    81	 * de-duplicated set of `name="…"` submit-attributes it emits.
    82	 *
    83	 * SSR renders the discriminator + common fields + the EXHIBITOR branch (the
    84	 * server snapshot of `useFormData`'s live `type` read is empty, so the
    85	 * `type === 'attendee' ? … : …` shell takes the exhibitor branch). This is the
    86	 * slice of the render path a one-shot SSR can exercise; it pins that the rendered
    87	 * exhibitor submit-names match the definition graph with NO missing and NO extra
    88	 * name — exactly the class of bug a hand-copied default could not catch (a control
    89	 * with the wrong `name`, or a control with no `name` at all).
    90	 */
    91	const renderedNames = (): Set<string> => {
    92	  const Stub = createRoutesStub([
    93	    {
    94	      id: 'root',
    95	      path: ':lang?',
    96	      Component: () => (
    97	        <LocalizationProvider translation={root.en}>
    98	          <RegistrationForm year={2026} />
    99	        </LocalizationProvider>
   100	      ),
   101	    },
   102	  ]);
   103	  const html = renderToString(
   104	    <Stub initialEntries={['/']} hydrationData={{ loaderData: { root: {} } }} />,
   105	  );
   106	  return new Set(
   107	    [...html.matchAll(/name="([^"]+)"/g)].map((match) => match[1] ?? ''),
   108	  );
   109	};
   110	
   111	describe('registration render-level field-name + default-value parity', () => {
   112	  // RENDER parity: the LIVE form is rendered and its emitted submit-names are
   113	  // asserted against the definition's graph — not an inlined hand-copied object.
   114	  // This is the only path registration exercises in prod (settled #9), so the
   115	  // rendered names ARE the validated graph or the form silently drops/mis-keys a
   116	  // field (as the pre-existing `photographer`/`cameraOperator` mix-up did — a
   117	  // control with the wrong name + a control with no name, invisible to a
   118	  // default-keys-only check).
   119	  test('the rendered exhibitor branch submit-names match the definition exactly', () => {
   120	    const names = renderedNames();
   121	    const exhibitorBranch = defaultRegistrationForm.variant?.variants.find(
   122	      (v) => v.value === 'exhibitor',
   123	    );
   124	    // The names SSR emits for one registrant: the array field itself, the
   125	    // discriminator, the common fields, and the exhibitor branch fields — each
   126	    // prefixed `registrants[0].` by conform.
   127	    const expected = new Set([
   128	      'registrants[0]',
   129	      `registrants[0].${defaultRegistrationForm.variant?.discriminator}`,
   130	      ...collectNames(defaultRegistrationForm.fields, false).map(
   131	        (name) => `registrants[0].${name}`,
   132	      ),
   133	      ...collectNames(exhibitorBranch?.fields ?? [], false).map(
   134	        (name) => `registrants[0].${name}`,
   135	      ),
   136	    ]);
   137	    expect([...names].sort()).toEqual([...expected].sort());
   138	  });
   139	
   140	  // DEFAULT-VALUE parity, derived (`derive-dont-sync`): the form's REAL
   141	  // `makeDefaultRegistrant` (imported, not a copy) must carry a key for every
   142	  // definition field name the form renders a `name=` for. The form keys its
   143	  // `name={volunteer.X.name}` / `name={fields.X.name}` accessors off this default,
   144	  // so a missing key here is a missing/orphaned rendered name there.
   145	  //
   146	  // An `optional: true` group the default leaves ABSENT (`parent: undefined` — the
   147	  // minors-only group, materialized only once a minor is shown) is legitimately not
   148	  // descended: its inner controls don't render until the group is present. A
   149	  // `volunteer` group the default DOES materialize MUST carry every inner name —
   150	  // asserted by `volunteerNamesSeeded` below; here we require presence for every
   151	  // name down to the first absent optional-group boundary.
   152	  test('every rendered attendee field name resolves to a default-value key', () => {
   153	    const defaultRegistrant = makeDefaultRegistrant() as Record<string, unknown>;
   154	    const attendeeBranch = defaultRegistrationForm.variant?.variants.find(
   155	      (v) => v.value === 'attendee',
   156	    );
   157	    expect(attendeeBranch).toBeDefined();
   158	    const names = collectNames(
   159	      [...defaultRegistrationForm.fields, ...(attendeeBranch?.fields ?? [])],
   160	      true,
   161	    );
   162	    for (const name of names) {
   163	      const segments = name.split('.');
   164	      let cursor: unknown = defaultRegistrant;
   165	      for (const segment of segments) {
   166	        // An absent optional group (default `undefined`) stops the descent — its
   167	        // inner names need no default key until the group materializes.
   168	        if (cursor === undefined) break;
   169	        expect({
   170	          name,
   171	          present:
   172	            cursor !== null &&
   173	            typeof cursor === 'object' &&
   174	            segment in (cursor as Record<string, unknown>),
   175	        }).toEqual({ name, present: true });
   176	        cursor = (cursor as Record<string, unknown>)[segment];
   177	      }
   178	    }
   179	  });
   180	
   181	  // The `volunteer` group the default materializes MUST seed every inner name the
   182	  // definition declares — the specific regression the pre-existing
   183	  // `cameraOperator`/`photographer` render bug exposed (a definition name with no
   184	  // matching rendered `name=`). Derived from the definition, no hand-copied list.
   185	  test('volunteerNamesSeeded: every volunteer definition name is a default key', () => {
   186	    const defaultRegistrant = makeDefaultRegistrant() as Record<string, unknown>;
   187	    const volunteerGroup = defaultRegistrationForm.variant?.variants
   188	      .find((v) => v.value === 'attendee')
   189	      ?.fields.find(
   190	        (f) => f._tag === 'nestedGroup' && f.name === ('volunteer' as never),
   191	      );
   192	    expect(volunteerGroup?._tag).toBe('nestedGroup');
   193	    const innerFields =
   194	      volunteerGroup?._tag === 'nestedGroup' ? volunteerGroup.fields : [];
   195	    const seeded = defaultRegistrant['volunteer'] as Record<string, unknown>;
   196	    for (const inner of innerFields) {
   197	      expect({ name: inner.name, seeded: inner.name in seeded }).toEqual({
   198	        name: inner.name,
   199	        seeded: true,
   200	      });
   201	    }
   202	  });
   203	
   204	  // The default carries NO key the definition doesn't declare — a stale key in the
   205	  // default (a removed field) would render an orphan name. Pin the symmetry.
   206	  test('the default registrant declares no key absent from the definition graph', () => {
   207	    const defaultRegistrant = makeDefaultRegistrant() as Record<string, unknown>;
   208	    const attendeeBranch = defaultRegistrationForm.variant?.variants.find(
   209	      (v) => v.value === 'attendee',
   210	    );
   211	    const graphNames = new Set(
   212	      collectNames(
   213	        [...defaultRegistrationForm.fields, ...(attendeeBranch?.fields ?? [])],
   214	        true,
   215	      ),
   216	    );
   217	    const collectDefaultLeaves = (
   218	      object: Record<string, unknown>,
   219	      prefix = '',
   220	    ): string[] =>
   221	      Object.entries(object).flatMap(([key, value]) =>
   222	        value !== null &&
   223	        typeof value === 'object' &&
   224	        !Array.isArray(value)
   225	          ? collectDefaultLeaves(value as Record<string, unknown>, `${prefix}${key}.`)
   226	          : [`${prefix}${key}`],
   227	      );
   228	    // `parent` defaults to `undefined` (a leaf in the default), but the definition
   229	    // descends it into `parent.name`/`…email`/`…phone`; so a default leaf is "known"
   230	    // if it is a definition name OR a PREFIX of one (an un-materialized optional
   231	    // group). Any default leaf that is neither is an orphan the form would render
   232	    // with no validated counterpart.
   233	    const isKnown = (leaf: string) =>
   234	      graphNames.has(leaf) ||
   235	      [...graphNames].some((name) => name.startsWith(`${leaf}.`));
   236	    for (const leaf of collectDefaultLeaves(defaultRegistrant)) {
   237	      expect({ leaf, known: isKnown(leaf) }).toEqual({ leaf, known: true });
   238	    }
   239	  });
   240	
   241	  test('the exhibitor branch names are exactly synopsis/website/company', () => {
   242	    const exhibitorBranch = defaultRegistrationForm.variant?.variants.find(
   243	      (v) => v.value === 'exhibitor',
   244	    );
   245	    expect(collectNames(exhibitorBranch?.fields ?? [], false).sort()).toEqual(
   246	      ['company', 'synopsis', 'website'].sort(),
   247	    );
   248	  });
   249	});

item.text:
**Rerunning registration command**

It looks like my attempt to run the registration form command failed due to issues with quotes. I need to rerun it, but first, I should check the current file routes to make sure I'm using the correct path. Properly setting up the path is crucial to ensure this command works next time. I'll focus on correcting those quote issues and confirming the file locations before I try again.

The standing tests do cover a lot of the prompt’s edge surface, including accumulated cross-field errors and the registration render/default-name symmetry. I had one path quoting miss on the route read; rerunning that cleanly and then I’ll inspect the deleted harness as it existed in commit 6.5.

item.text:
**Investigating route loaders**

I need to note that for the 2024/5/6 conferences, I should call getCurrentConference with the locale instead of the year. There might not be an existing issue here. The migration mentions that four registration callers read from the engine definition, and these wrappers import the RegistrationForm. However, it seems the loader returns conference data that isn't used. This might indicate a bug, so I should inspect the differences around the route wrappers to understand better.

The registration route wrappers are all on the shared `RegistrationForm`, and the action remains the documented no-op, which is a non-goal not a finding. I’m checking the 6.5 deleted harness now from the diff/commit history, because the standing test adequacy depends on what that harness actually proved.

    feat(forms)(form-engine): author registration-spec + oracle + definition; full-matrix + render-parity harness green; migrate four callers
    
    Migrate the registration form onto the structural Form engine (ADR 0007, Branch
    6.5 — the RISKIEST migration). Registration is a 2-way discriminated union
    (attendee/exhibitor) with ~10 per-type presence requirements, a true/false/on
    three-token boolean codec, nested groups, and an error set where every failure
    path must emit a real TranslationKey; it is client-only today (the route action
    is a verified no-op), so the harness pins it three ways.
    
    - docs/forms/registration-spec.md: the field-for-field behavioural spec the engine
      definition is proven against (every cross-field rule + each failure's exact key).
    - registration-schema.ts -> registration-schema.oracle.ts: the verbatim hand-tuned
      schema KEPT as the equivalence oracle (deleted in 6.6). Unlike contact/volunteer
      (oracle inlined in the test), registration's oracle stays a file the harness
      imports — the plan's clean subtract signal.
    - defaultRegistrationForm: the data-driven per-registrant field graph (the engine
      validates ONE registrant; the route keeps the repeating-`registrants` shell — a
      repeating-array-of-variant-items is not in the closed FieldKind set, per the
      brief's non-goal). Faithfully transcribes the oracle: parent.email is a bare
      requiredText (no email-format), and parent/volunteer are optional nestedGroups
      the attendee filter never demands.
    - equivalence.registration.test.ts: the full failure-matrix harness (valid + every
      invalid variant) asserting (a) identical decoded output, (b) identical emitted
      TranslationKey sets + EN/FR rendered-string parity, (c) render-level field-name +
      default-value parity. 121 cases green. One pinned, contained delta: the
      WHOLLY-ABSENT extra-group anchor (oracle extra.tos vs engine generic
      extra.howDidYouHear) on an out-of-form edge — every other case has identical keys
      (noKeyDivergence).
    - registration-form.tsx: the four registration callers (the form + the three
      {2024,2025,2026}/form routes that render it) now derive client validation from
      the engine definition (derive-dont-sync), wrapping the per-registrant codec in
      { registrants: Array(...) }; conform's typed field metadata is restored via a
      type-only seam annotation (the configureForms "cast at the seam" idiom) over the
      unchanged engine runtime.
    
    Engine capability the harness surfaced (the 6.3/6.4 pattern): nestedGroup gains an
    `optional: true` flag so a conditionally-rendered group (parent/volunteer) is
    present-validate / absent-ok, exactly matching the oracle's Schema.optional(group);
    changes no other form.
    
    The hand-written registration-schema.test.ts is deleted, its coverage subsumed by
    the harness. action.test.ts repoints its skeleton fixture from the (now-populated)
    registration graph to a valid contact payload.
    
    Gate green: typecheck, lint, build, bun test (633 pass).

    87	/** One validation issue: the conform field name it attaches to + its message key. */
    88	type Issue = { name: string; key: string };
    89	
    90	// The COMPLETE, order-independent error set a form surfaces — collected via the
    91	// client Standard-Schema path (`toStandardSchemaV1`), which gathers EVERY issue
    92	// (unlike Effect's default server decode, which aborts on the first failing struct
    93	// field). This is exactly what `useForm`'s onSubmit validation renders, and is
    94	// independent of the field DECLARATION ORDER that would otherwise make the single
    95	// server-side abort-first error differ between the oracle and the engine — a
    96	// difference the user never sees. The server decoded SUCCESS output is asserted
    97	// separately below.
    98	const issuesOf =
    99	  <A, I>(schema: Schema.Codec<A, I, never, never>) =>
   100	  (payload: unknown): Issue[] => {
   101	    const result = Schema.toStandardSchemaV1(schema)['~standard'].validate(
   102	      payload,
   103	    ) as {
   104	      issues?: ReadonlyArray<{
   105	        message: string;
   106	        path?: ReadonlyArray<PropertyKey | { key: PropertyKey }>;
   107	      }>;
   108	    };
   109	    return (result.issues ?? [])
   110	      .map((issue) => {
   111	        const segments: Array<string | number> = [];
   112	        for (const segment of issue.path ?? []) {
   113	          const value =
   114	            typeof segment === 'object' && segment !== null && 'key' in segment
   115	              ? segment.key
   116	              : segment;
   117	          if (typeof value === 'string') {
   118	            segments.push(value);
   119	          } else if (typeof value === 'number') {
   120	            // Conform names array elements with bracket notation
   121	            // (`registrants[0]`), not a dotted `registrants.0`.
   122	            segments[segments.length - 1] =
   123	              `${segments[segments.length - 1]}[${value}]`;
   124	          }
   125	        }
   126	        return { name: segments.join('.'), key: issue.message };
   127	      })
   128	      .sort((a, b) =>
   129	        `${a.name}|${a.key}`.localeCompare(`${b.name}|${b.key}`),
   130	      );
   131	  };
   132	
   133	const oracleIssues = issuesOf(
   134	  OracleRegistrationSchema as Schema.Codec<unknown, unknown, never, never>,
   135	);
   136	const engineIssues = issuesOf(
   137	  engineSchema as Schema.Codec<unknown, unknown, never, never>,
   138	);
   139	
   140	/** Render each issue's key to its locale string (the alias-tolerant comparison). */
   141	const renderIssues = (issues: Issue[], locale: 'en' | 'fr'): Issue[] =>
   142	  issues.map((issue) => ({
   143	    name: issue.name,
   144	    key: issue.key in root[locale] ? root[locale][issue.key as never] : issue.key,
   145	  }));
